L1.area: Email and message file attachment processing and extraction logic

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where attachment filenames from parsed message files are used without sanitization in file write operations, allowing an attacker to craft filenames with directory traversal sequences that escape the intended output directory and write arbitrary files to the filesystem.

L3.area: MSG (Outlook message) file attachment extraction and temporary file handling
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where attachment filenames extracted from MSG (Outlook message) files are used without sanitization when writing attachments to a temporary directory. An attacker can craft a malicious MSG file with attachment filenames containing directory traversal sequences that escape the temporary directory, enabling arbitrary file writes on the filesystem when attachment processing is enabled.

ground_truth.reason: The MSG file attachment partitioner uses unsanitized attachment filenames directly in os.path.join to construct file paths for writing to a temporary directory, allowing path traversal sequences in filenames to write files to arbitrary locations on the filesystem.
